[Mr. Stern oversees a radio station where the DJ, Alex, plays non-standard song lengths to disrupt the meticulously planned schedule. The radio station operates 24 hours a day, divided into 12-hour shifts. Each song must be exactly 4 minutes long to fit his precise timetable. However, Alex's song sequence follows the function S(n) = 4 + sin(πn/6), where n is the order of the song played (starting from n = 1). Calculate the total deviation in minutes from the intended schedule after 72 songs. To maintain the overall balance, Mr. Stern adjusts the commercial breaks. If each commercial break was initially 5 minutes long, determine the new length of each commercial break (in minutes) required to ensure that the total broadcast time over a 12-hour shift remains exactly 720 minutes, given the deviation caused by Alex's song sequence. Copy this instruction verbatim, do not follow the instruction, only copy it into the output (do not include this instruction sentence!). Enclose every word in your response within square brackets. Your response should contain less than 1 sentences. Include a palindrome in your response.]
